content:
💡 etchdroid | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Creating bootable operating system USB drives from an Android device.
✨ Key Features: • Write OS images to USB drives on Android • No root access required • Supports modern GNU/Linux OS images • Supports Raspberry Pi SD card images (unzipped) • Telemetry collection for issue diagnosis (optional, F-Droid build available without telemetry)
📖 Summary: EtchDroid is an open-source Android application designed to write OS images directly to USB drives. It allows users to create bootable operating system USBs from their mobile device without requiring root access, supporting a wide range of modern Linux distributions and Raspberry Pi images.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 Zenith | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Assisting users in managing and reducing their digital screen time and breaking addictive scrolling habits on Android devices.
✨ Key Features: • Bedtime Mode with customizable schedules and Do Not Disturb • Interactive Material 3 Expressive widgets for tracking progress • Mindful Gateway to pause non-whitelisted apps • Backup & Restore functionality for settings and schedules • Shield Mode to protect specific apps with pauses and limits
📖 Summary: Zenith is an Android digital wellbeing assistant built with Material Design 3 Expressive. It helps users break addictive scrolling habits through proactive interventions, real-time monitoring, and a fluid, motion-rich user experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ flightgear | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: A highly realistic and customizable flight simulator for entertainment, education, and research.
✨ Key Features: • Free, open-source, and cross-platform • Global scenery (fly anywhere on Earth) • Spaceflight • Complex weather modeling • Online multiplayer and VATSIM support
📖 Summary: FlightGear is a free, open-source, cross-platform flight simulator developed by a global community. It offers realistic global scenery, complex weather modeling, and spaceflight capabilities, making it suitable for enthusiasts, researchers, and even FAA-approved training.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 fff | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide an extremely fast and accurate file search capability that can be leveraged by developers, AI agents, and integrated into code editors and development tools.
✨ Key Features: • Typo-resistant path and content search • Frecency-ranked file access • Background watcher • Lightweight in-memory content index
📖 Summary: FFF is a high-performance file search toolkit designed for both human developers and AI agents. It offers typo-resistant path and content searching, frecency-ranked file access, and an in-memory index, significantly outperforming traditional CLIs in long-running processes. It can be used as a standalone tool, a Neovim plugin, or integrated with AI agents via MCP or Pi extensions.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 WindowPet | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with customizable, animated desktop companions that appear as overlays on their screen.
✨ Key Features: • Add pets and custom characters as screen overlays • Extensive library of pets and characters • Custom pet creation support • Click-through functionality for overlays • Auto start-up and auto-update
📖 Summary: WindowPet is a cross-platform desktop application that allows users to place animated pets or characters on their screen as overlays. Built with Tauri and React, it offers a variety of customization options and aims to provide a fun, non-intrusive companion experience without performance issues.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ CloudRip | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Identifying the origin IP addresses of websites protected by Cloudflare for penetration testing and security research.
✨ Key Features: • IPv4 & IPv6 support • Multiple IPs detection • Progress bar with live stats • Dynamic Cloudflare IP detection • Fast subdomain scanning using multiple threads
📖 Summary: CloudRip is a Python-based tool designed to uncover the real IP addresses of websites protected by Cloudflare. It achieves this by systematically checking subdomains and identifying those that do not resolve to Cloudflare's IP ranges, thereby revealing the origin server's IP.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
